definitions.definition: Broad and fundamental truths that establish a relationship between cause and effect, serving as guidelines for managerial decision-making.
Dividing total work into smaller tasks, each assigned to a specialist, leading to greater efficiency and productivity.
An employee should receive orders from one and only one superior to avoid confusion and conflicting instructions.
One head and one plan for a group of activities having the same objective, ensuring coordinated effort.
The formal line of authority from the highest to the lowest rank. Can be bypassed through 'Gang Plank' in emergencies.
Promoting team spirit, unity and harmony among employees. 'Union is strength.'
Fair and just treatment of all employees by management, without discrimination or favouritism.
Taylor's approach: 'The art of knowing exactly what you want your men to do and seeing that they do it in the best and cheapest way.'
Taylor's concept where both management and workers change their attitudes toward each other — from conflict to cooperation.
Taylor's technique of separating planning from execution, with 8 specialised foremen (4 for planning, 4 for production).
Determining the standard time required to complete a specific task, used for planning, incentives, and labour cost calculation.
Analysing workers' movements to eliminate wasteful motions and improve efficiency.
A shorter route of communication where employees at the same level can communicate directly in emergencies, bypassing the scalar chain.
Taylor's wage system where efficient workers (above standard output) get higher per-unit wages than inefficient workers (below standard).

distinctions.between: Unity of Command vs Unity of Direction

points.b: One head, one plan for a group

scenarios.scenario: A worker at Tata Motors receives instructions from both the production manager and the quality manager, causing confusion.
All marketing activities of Hindustan Unilever are handled by one marketing head with a single marketing plan.
Workers at a steel plant are given rest intervals after every 2 hours of heavy work.
A company studies the movements of assembly line workers to remove unnecessary hand motions.
A manager ensures that raw materials are always stored in the correct warehouse section.
The CEO of a company shares profits with workers to maintain industrial peace.
In an emergency, two department heads communicate directly instead of going through the CEO.
Workers who produce 25+ units per day get ₹50/unit, while those producing fewer get ₹40/unit.
A bank manager encourages employees to come up with new ideas for improving customer service.
Management organises a team outing to build unity and team spirit among employees.
